en.extract:
The grey sunbird or mouse-coloured sunbird (Cyanomitra veroxii) is a species of bird in the family Nectariniidae.
It is native to shoreline areas of Africa, from Somalia to South Africa.


Diet
This species feeds on nectar. It has been recorded to feed on the nectar of Schotia brachypetala and Kigelia africana. It even engages in nectar robbing by piercing a hole in the side of the corolla. It is also a pollinator of 10 genera of plants with uses that are medicinal, edible or may be used as a building material.


References


External links
Species text in The Atlas of Southern African Birds.

The '''grey sunbird''' or '''mouse-coloured sunbird''' ('''''Cyanomitra veroxii''''') is a species of bird in the family [[Nectariniidae]].
It is native to shoreline areas of Africa, from [[Somalia]] to [[South Africa]].

==Diet==
This species feeds on nectar. It has been recorded to feed on the nectar of [[Schotia brachypetala]]<ref>{{cite journal |author=Symes, C. T. |author2=Yoganand, K. | journal=Bulletin of the African Bird Club | title=Schotia brachypetala - a nectar cornucopia for birds | volume=20 | issue=1 | pages=39–44 | date=1 January 2013 | url=https://www.researchgate.net/publication/248708244 | doi=10.5962/p.309992| doi-access=free }}</ref> and [[Kigelia africana]]. It even engages in [[nectar robbing]] by piercing a hole in the side of the corolla.<ref>{{cite journal |author=Newman, E. |author2=Govender, K. |author3=Van Niekerk, S. |author4=Johnson, S. D. | journal=Biotropica | title=The functional ecology of bat pollination in the African sausage tree Kigelia africana (Bignoniaceae) | volume=53 | issue=2 | pages=477–486 | date=2 February 2021 | url=https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/abs/10.1111/btp.12891 | doi=10.1111/btp.12891| bibcode=2021Biotr..53..477N | url-access=subscription }}</ref> It is also a pollinator of 10 genera of plants with uses that are medicinal, edible or may be used as a building material.<ref>{{cite journal |author=Newmark, W. D. |author2=Mkongewa, V. J. |author3=Amundsen, D. L. |author4=Welch, C. | journal=Ornithological Applications | title=African sunbirds predominantly pollinate plants useful to humans | volume=122 | issue=2 | date=21 December 2019 | article-number=duz070 | url=https://academic.oup.com/condor/article/122/2/duz070/5771327 | doi=10.1093/condor/duz070}}</ref>
